name: aligned_zero_size
reason: aligned with size 0 involves division by zero and should never hold; provability here indicates unsound handling of modulo-zero

name: alignment_doubles
code:
proof fn phi_2_alignment_doubles(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, b),
        b > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(a, 2 * b),
{
}
reason: alignment to b does not imply alignment to 2*b (e.g. aligned(4,4) but not aligned(4,8)); provability means the spec is too strong

name: shift_one_preserves_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_3_shift_one_preserves_alignment(a: nat, b: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, b),
        b > 1,
    ensures
        aligned((a + 1) as nat, b),
{
}
reason: adding 1 to an aligned address must break alignment for any size > 1; provability reveals a broken modular-arithmetic spec

name: product_size_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_4_product_size_alignment(a: nat, b: nat, c: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, b),
        aligned(a, c),
        b > 0,
        c > 0,
        b != c,
    ensures
        aligned(a, b * c),
{
}
reason: aligned to b and c individually does not imply aligned to b*c (e.g. aligned(12,4) and aligned(12,6) but not aligned(12,24)); provability signals a flawed compositionality assumption

name: nonmultiple_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_5_nonmultiple_aligned(b: nat)
    requires
        b > 1,
    ensures
        aligned(1, b),
{
}
reason: 1 is never a multiple of any b > 1 so aligned(1, b) must be false; provability means the spec incorrectly classifies non-aligned addresses as aligned
